title: Pump unit for feeding fuel to an internal-combustion engine and method for cooling said unit
abstract: A pump unit for feeding fuel, preferably diesel fuel, from a tank to an internal-combustion engine; the pump unit comprising: a low-pressure gear pump configured to draw fuel from the tank; a high-pressure piston pump fed by the low-pressure gear pump and configured to feed the fuel to the internal-combustion engine; an intake circuit connected upstream to the low-pressure gear pump and downstream to the high-pressure piston pump; a discharge circuit connected upstream to the intake circuit and downstream to the tank; a cooling circuit comprising in series a first portion connected upstream to the intake circuit or to the discharge circuit and downstream to the high-pressure piston pump and a second portion connected upstream to the high-pressure piston pump and downstream to the discharge circuit.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A pump unit (1) for feeding fuel, preferably diesel fuel, from a tank (2) to an internal-combustion engine (3); the pump unit (1) comprising: - a low-pressure gear pump (4) configured to draw fuel from the tank (2); - a high-pressure piston pump (5) fed by the low-pressure gear pump (4) and configured to feed the fuel to the internal-combustion engine (3); - an intake circuit (6) connected upstream to the low-pressure gear pump (4) and downstream to the high-pressure piston pump (5); - a discharge circuit (7) connected upstream to the intake circuit (6) and downstream to the tank (2); - a cooling circuit (8) comprising in series a first portion, connected upstream to the intake circuit (6) or to the discharge circuit (7) and downstream to the high- pressure piston pump, (5) and a second portion connected upstream to the high- pressure piston pump (5) and downstream to the discharge circuit (7). 2. Pump unit as claimed in Claim 1, wherein the high-pressure piston pump (5) comprises a mechanical intake valve (9), the first portion of the cooling circuit (8) being connected upstream to the intake circuit (6). 3. Pump unit as claimed in Claim 2, wherein the intake circuit (6) comprises a metering valve (10), the first portion of the cooling circuit (8) being connected upstream to the intake circuit (6) and upstream of the metering valve (10). 4. Pump unit as claimed in Claim 1, wherein the high-pressure piston pump (5) comprises an electrical intake valve (11), the first portion of the cooling circuit (8) being connected upstream to the discharge circuit (7). 5. Pump unit as claimed in Claim 4, wherein the discharge circuit (7) comprises an overflow valve (12), the first portion of the cooling circuit (8) being 

 connected upstream to the overflow valve (12) and downstream to the discharge circuit (7) downstream of the overflow valve (12). 6. Pump unit as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the piston pump comprises: - a head (13) inside which a cylinder (14) is formed along an axis (A); and - a pumping piston (15) housed inside the cylinder (14); wherein the cooling circuit (8) comprises an annular feed header (16) and an annular discharge header (17) which are arranged annularly and at different heights with respect to the axis (A), respectively downstream of the first portion and upstream of the second portion of the cooling circuit (8), the two annular headers (16, 17) being connected to a bottom portion of the cylinder for collecting the seeping fuel respectively by a feed channel (18) and a discharge channel (19). 7. Pump unit as claimed in Claim 6, wherein the pump unit (1) comprises at least two high-pressure piston pumps (5), the two annular feed headers (16) and the two annular discharge headers (17) being connected together by intermediate portions (20, 21) of the cooling circuit. 8. Pump unit as claimed in Claim 6 or 7, wherein a sealing ring (22) is present in the bottom portion of the cylinder (14) for collecting the seeping fuel. 9. Method for cooling a pump unit (1) for feeding fuel, preferably diesel fuel, from a tank (2) to an internal-combustion engine (3), the method comprising the following steps: - providing a pump unit (1) according to any one of the preceding claims; - drawing off part of the fuel passing through the intake circuit (6) or in the discharge circuit (7); - feeding the drawn-off fuel to the cylinder (14) of the high-pressure piston pump (5); - evacuating from the cylinder (14) of the high-pressure piston pump (15) the drawn-off fuel together with the fuel seeping along the cylinder (14); - discharging the drawn-off fuel together with the fuel seeping along the cylinder 

 (14) along the discharge circuit (7). 10. Method as claimed in Claim 9, wherein the pump unit (1) comprises a plurality of high-pressure piston pumps (5); the step of feeding the drawn-off fuel to the cylinder (14) of the high-pressure piston pump (5) and evacuating from the cylinder (14) of the high-pressure piston pump (15) the drawn-off fuel together with the fuel seeping along the cylinder (14) are performed in parallel.
